title: malus coronaria var. bracteata
intro: Malus coronaria var. bracteata, commonly known as the Rock Crabapple or Bracted Crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent qualities. The health benefits of Malus coronaria var. bracteata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
